seoTitle: West Gladstone vs West Haldon — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between West Gladstone and West Haldon. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: West Gladstone vs West Haldon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in West Gladstone and West Haldon.
heading: West Gladstone vs West Haldon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between West Gladstone, Queensland and West Haldon, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: West Gladstone has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to West Haldon (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is West Gladstone or West Haldon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: West Gladstone has a flood risk score of 35/100, while West Haldon scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: West Gladstone scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. West Haldon scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in West Gladstone or West Haldon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. West Gladstone has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
